title: Red Sox took advantage of MLB’s new automated strike challenge rules on Opening Day
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox utilized MLB's new Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System effectively in a 3-0 win against the Reds on Opening Day.
Catcher Carlos Narvaez's successful challenge in the fourth inning led to a crucial reversal favoring Boston's pitcher Garrett Crochet.
Roman Anthony's ninth-inning challenge overturned a strike call, leading to additional runs and securing Boston's victory.

title: In first Opening Day with Red Sox, Marcelo Mayer made quite the impression off the bench
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Marcelo Mayer contributed significantly in his first Opening Day with the Red Sox, going 2-for-2 with two runs.
Mayer's performance included a key seventh-inning double that led to Boston's first run, breaking a 0-0 tie.
Despite a challenging previous season, Mayer earned his roster spot and impressed off the bench in a 3-0 win.

title: Lucky pup thanks NH firefighters for quick thinking that saved his life
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Ziggy the dog was injured by a solar light and bled severely while his owners were on vacation.
The sitter sought help at Campton-Thornton Fire Department; firefighters applied a tourniquet and stopped the bleeding.
Veterinarian confirmed firefighters' actions saved Ziggy's life; he received surgery and is recovering well.

title: What’s causing my car to burn so much oil?
author: John Paul
bullets: John Paul advises addressing a Subaru Legacy's excessive oil consumption, suggesting an engine flush and PCV system check.
A reader questioned a $670 quote for a Toyota Camry's water pump replacement, which John Paul finds reasonable.
John Paul recommends a 1.6L turbocharged Hyundai Kona over the 2.0L version for a sportier driving experience.

title: This Nantucket diner just sold its fifth million dollar prize in the last 2 years
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Old South Diner in Nantucket sold its fifth $1 million+ lottery ticket since March 2024, state officials reported.
The diner recently sold two $2 million tickets in March 2026, including a Powerball prize.
The diner receives a $20,000 bonus for each winning ticket sold, per Massachusetts State Lottery Commission.

title: School bus driver in crash that killed child was allegedly involved in hit-and-run months later
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jean Charles, a former Boston school bus driver, faces charges of involuntary manslaughter and vehicle homicide for a crash that killed a 5-year-old boy.
Charles is also facing charges for allegedly being involved in an August 2025 hit-and-run incident while driving with a suspended license.
Investigations revealed Charles' negligence and expired certification, prompting a safety review of Boston Public Schools' transportation contractor, Transdev.

title: 5 takeaways as Garrett Crochet, Marcelo Mayer steer Red Sox to 3-0 Opening Day win over Reds
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Garrett Crochet secures Opening Day win for Red Sox, striking out key Reds hitters in the sixth inning.
Pinch hitter Marcelo Mayer drives crucial run, sparking Boston's seventh-inning momentum shift and adding another hit in the ninth.
Roman Anthony shines with three hits, maintaining momentum and contributing to Boston's 12-hit effort in 3-0 victory.

title: Body of Mass. man missing since escaping from a hospital in 2025 found in lake
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Leonard Mercury, 57, missing since October 2025, was found dead in Crystal Lake, Gardner, officials confirmed.
Mercury disappeared after escaping Heywood Hospital in Gardner, leaving via a broken window without shoes.
Gardner police discovered Mercury using drones; State Police Dive Team retrieved the body from the lake.

title: A doctor once told her not to run. Now she’s taking on the Boston Marathon.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Sydney Southern, 33, from Peabody, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon over 10 years after leg surgery at MGH.
Southern was advised to avoid running after a femur tumor surgery but has been cleared for full activity now.
She's running to raise funds for Mass General Hospital, which provided her with care and support during recovery.

title: Cambridge pediatric doctor running fifth Boston Marathon to support Mass General patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Jennifer Leah Goetz from Cambridge is running her fifth Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital patients.
Goetz, a 39-year-old pediatric doctor, started running to manage depression and gained a deeper purpose through her work.
Her marathon participation helps raise funds for MGH, inspired by her experiences with patients battling cancer.
